answer.recommended_action: h2 (laser aging) is confirmed as the primary cause: ev.laser_power_check_001 documents a sustained −5.5% source-side emitter power loss (45.4 mW vs 48.1 mW baseline), which is physically consistent with the observed range degradation. However, this power loss alone accounts for only ~2–4% range reduction, leaving a residual ~4–6% gap unexplained — h3 (detector responsivity drift) remains a viable co-contributor and h4 (TEC/thermal degradation as the mechanism driving the laser power loss) remains open. Two actions are required in priority order: (1) Measure per-channel APD responsivity and bias to quantify h3 and close the residual range budget; (2) Retrieve per-channel TEC setpoint-vs-actual junction-temperature logs to determine whether h4 is the root mechanism behind the laser power decline (thermal excursion → laser depression) versus intrinsic end-of-life aging — this distinction is critical: h4 implies TEC repair may partially restore output, while pure h2 aging implies laser replacement. The staggered 4-day onset spread (ev.common_mode_check_001) and the coincident-but-non-causal reboot (ev.trigger_demoted) confirm per-channel independent drift and rule out any single shared upstream event.

hypotheses.label: Window contamination (external/internal fouling reducing transmitted power)
Laser aging / output power decline reducing emitted energy budget
Detector responsivity drift reducing receive sensitivity
Thermal/TEC degradation causing operating-temperature excursion that depresses laser or detector performance
Calibration / signal-processing drift biasing range threshold or SNR estimate downward

evidence.summary: 2 channels are degraded with a 4-day onset spread; the check explicitly returns common_mode=false. Staggered per-channel onset argues against any single simultaneous shared cause (upstream power, shared thermal event, or shared calibration stage). Independently-drifting per-channel components (window fouling, laser aging, detector drift) are consistent with this pattern.

Laser output power measured at 45.4 mW vs 48.1 mW baseline (−5.5%), confirmed as a sustained, source-side emitter power loss. Physical decline at the emitter directly implicates laser aging (h2) or thermally-induced laser depression (h4). Because the measurement is at the source (before any optical window), window fouling (h1) cannot account for this reading — h1 as a sole/primary cause is weakened. A 5.5% emitter power loss translates to a partial range penalty (≈2–4% range reduction depending on scaling regime), leaving a residual ~4–6% range gap unexplained and keeping h3 (detector drift) viable as a co-contributor rather than fully exonerated. The result is a real physical decline, arguing against h5 (calibration/processing threshold artifact). The per-channel, source-localized nature further corroborates rejection of a shared upstream cause (hyp.upstream_power).
log.reboot: degradation onset predates it → coincident, not causal

trace.rationale: seed the differential from the symptom

With all six hypotheses at equal prior and zero evidence collected, the highest-leverage first move is to determine whether the ~8 % range loss is confined to a single channel/aperture or appears simultaneously across multiple channels. A positive common-mode result would strongly raise hyp.upstream_power (shared laser bus, power-supply rail, or scanner optics) while depressing h1–h5 (which are each localized component failures). A negative result — degradation on only one channel — eliminates hyp.upstream_power and focuses the differential squarely on h1–h5. No other available check bifurcates the hypothesis space this cleanly without prior evidence: laser_power_check or tec_load_check would only probe one leaf of the tree at a time, whereas common_mode_check answers 'systemic vs. isolated' in a single step.

h1, h2, and h3 are all tied at log_odds=0.00. The single cleanest split is to measure per-channel emitted laser power directly. If laser output on the degraded channels is nominally on-spec, h2 (laser aging) is substantially ruled out and the field narrows to h1 (window fouling—external loss before the receiver) vs h3 (detector responsivity drift—internal receive-side loss). If laser power is measurably depressed on the same channels that show range degradation (with onset alignment), h2 is strongly implicated and h1/h3 recede. This single measurement therefore moves h2 in the opposite direction from h1/h3, maximising separation. It also partially informs h4: a laser power drop correlated with TEC load or temperature would reopen h4. No laser-power evidence has yet been retrieved (ev.common_mode_check_001 is the only evidence on record and addresses only the shared-cause axis, not the transmit/receive/window axis).
order onset vs the salient recent event; sanity-check channels
conclude or abstain from the differential
